title: My LLM based quant and operator desk at work - Week 23 game plan
author: Busy-Specialist7708
selftext:
https://preview.redd.it/8gypjb3hst5h1.png?width=1536&format=png&auto=webp&s=5af0a0810c6ddc842496f5db890f1621ccd6c91d

https://preview.redd.it/0aljnowhst5h1.png?width=1672&format=png&auto=webp&s=0a1189298b9de3c30970e03fa73561e46905440c

Image 1 = Quant Desk briefing

Image 2 = Operator Desk briefing

comments.author: ElbowlessGoat
comments.body: Looks great, what kind of work do you do?

replies.author: Busy-Specialist7708
replies.body: Well, that's an odd and personal question to ask. I deliver stuff, so I'm on the road all day with no boss or snitch around me that can complain when I take a break to chart and analyze stuff 😂

replies.author: ElbowlessGoat
replies.body: Haha, no worries. I was wondering how the dashboards fit into your work life. I was convinced you had a WFH desk job. 😂

replies.author: Busy-Specialist7708
replies.body: I work parttime and also always in weekends, so I got 3,5 day off in the intraweek to build my early pension 😂

replies.author: ElbowlessGoat
replies.body: If at any point you are willing to share the architecture behind it, I am willing to take notes. I understand it if you don’t want to.

replies.author: Busy-Specialist7708
replies.body:
Another bold question 😂

Idk why I should do that. It's more like 25 files with frameworks, protocols and master prompts. Spends months creating and fine tuning the workflow. Started with a forex ai enhanced system, which gave me a lot of headaches. Then some random guy on reddit expects me to hand it over, lol
